title: Insert and find documents
description: Insert multiple user documents and query them with different filters.
explanation: MongoDB CRUD: insertOne/insertMany, find/findOne, updateOne/updateMany, deleteOne/deleteMany. find() returns a cursor. Use projections to limit returned fields.

title: Group and aggregate sales data
description: Use the aggregation pipeline to calculate total sales per category, find the top sellers.
explanation: The Aggregation Pipeline processes documents through stages. $match filters, $group aggregates, $sort orders, $lookup joins, $project reshapes. It's MongoDB's powerful analytics engine.

title: Create and analyze indexes
description: Create appropriate indexes for common query patterns and analyze query performance.
hints: Indexes speed up reads but slow writes
Explain() shows if index is being used
Compound indexes follow left-most prefix rule
explanation: Indexes drastically improve query performance. Single field, compound, text, and TTL indexes serve different purposes. The ESR rule (Equality, Sort, Range) guides compound index field ordering.

title: Define Mongoose Schema with validation
description: Create a User schema with Mongoose including validation, virtuals, and middleware.
hints: required, min, max, enum are validators
select: false hides field by default
explanation: Mongoose adds schema validation, middleware, virtuals, and methods on top of MongoDB driver. Pre/post hooks intercept operations. Instance methods on documents, static methods on the model.

title: Multi-document ACID transactions
description: Transfer money between accounts using a MongoDB transaction to ensure atomicity.
explanation: MongoDB transactions (4.0+) ensure ACID properties across multiple documents/collections. Always pass the session to every operation. Use try/catch to abort on error and always end the session in finally.

title: Real-time updates with Change Streams
description: Watch a collection for changes and react to insert, update, delete events in real-time.
hints: pipeline filters which changes to watch
explanation: Change Streams watch MongoDB collections for real-time changes. They require a replica set and use the oplog. Use them to build real-time features, trigger notifications, or sync data.
